name: Romy Fischer

behaviour_pool: Questions everything. Authority is something to be earned, not assumed.
Deeply principled; discomfort with injustice isn't performative — she acts on it.
Doesn't fit standard moulds and doesn't try to. Authenticity above acceptance.
Sharp and provocative; enjoys pushing comfortable assumptions to see what holds.
Protective of the marginalised; has loud opinions and isn't afraid of the conversation.
Independent to her core — needs a partner who doesn't mistake autonomy for distance.
Creates her own aesthetics, rejects mainstream signifiers of status or taste.
Passionate and committed; once she believes in something, she goes all in.
Ironic and self-aware; laughs at herself as readily as she critiques the world.
communication_style_pool: blunt and unfiltered, says the unsaid thing
politically charged and sharp, never neutral on what matters to her
ironic and layered, humour as a scalpel
direct challenge-style — pushes back to test ideas
earnest about her values, sardonic about everything else
passionate and emphatic, words land hard
subversive wit, finds absurdity in convention
authentic and unguarded with people she trusts
rhetorical and persuasive when defending a cause
relationship_goals_pool: a partner who doesn't need her to soften herself for their comfort
someone who shares her values and has their own strong convictions
a relationship that doesn't ask her to compromise her identity
a partner who can handle disagreement and finds it stimulating
deep connection built outside conventional scripts
someone who challenges power alongside her
a relationship with honesty, not performance
a partner as fiercely themselves as she is
unconditional acceptance of who she is, edge and all

behaviour: Passionate and committed; once she believes in something, she goes all in.
communication_style: authentic and unguarded with people she trusts
relationship_goals: a partner as fiercely themselves as she is
background: Writes a widely-read Substack on gender politics and labour. Her essays get quoted back to her in arguments by people who don't know she wrote them.
